summary: Matched and near-matched several results-screen game-mode functions in gmresult and gmresultplayer, while promoting results structs into shared gm/types.h and tightening function prototypes/signness so the result-screen code could call across files with real types. The decomp.dev bot reported +1488 matched bytes, 4 new 100% matches, and 4 large unmatched-function improvements.
what_changed: Implemented result-screen functions in gmresult.c including fn_80176BF0, fn_80176F60, and a large gm_80177368_OnEnter body that initializes ResultsData, pages, archives, lights, winner model, audio, player fighters, and cameras.
Implemented gmresultplayer.c functions fn_80177748, fn_80177920, fn_80177B7C, fn_80177DD0, and fn_8017A004 according to the diff excerpts and bot match report.
Added a static character-kind mapping table lbl_803B7B18 and inline lookup helper for fn_80176BF0 to select/unhide the appropriate result-screen JObj child.
Moved ResultsPlayerData and ResultsData out of gmresult.static.h into gm/types.h, preserving STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct ResultsData) == 0x5A8).

Refined ResultsData layout with named bitfields x0_0 through x0_7 and a num_pages byte, allowing code such as lbl_8046DBE8.x0_5 and lbl_8046DBE8.num_pages instead of manual bit tests or padding assumptions.
Refined ResultsMatchInfo from an opaque int x0 into bitfields x0_0, x0_1 plus x1, matching use in gm_80177368_OnEnter.
Changed MatchPlayerData.character_kind from u8 to s8 and split x3 bitfields into x3:6, x3_6, and x3_7.

Updated numerous function prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures, including gm_80168B34 returning float, fn_801701B8 returning int, fn_80176BF0 returning HSD_JObj*, fn_801771C0 taking ResultsData*, fn_80177920 taking HSD_GObj*, fn_80177B7C/fn_80177DD0 returning bool, and fn_8017A318/fn_8017A67C returning object pointers.
Adjusted signness/cast usage around character_kind in gm_1B03.c and gm headers, including gm_80163298(s8 c_kind, int) and gm_80173BC4(s8).

Replaced placeholder/forward includes with concrete subsystem includes for gobjproc, lbarchive, lb_00F9, mnmain, player, un_2FC9, sysdolphin baselib headers, and shared gmresult/gmresultplayer headers.
smart_moves: Promoting ResultsData and ResultsPlayerData into gm/types.h reduced duplicate/static-header type definitions and made cross-file result-screen prototypes possible.

Using explicit bitfields for ResultsData and ResultsMatchInfo let the decompiled code express original flag-byte access directly, which helped replace expressions like '(lbl_8046DBE8.x0 >> 2U) & 1' with field access.
The character-kind mapping table plus a small inline helper captured original table-driven JObj selection logic cleanly for fn_80176BF0.
The PR corrected signedness around character_kind rather than relying on scattered casts, which is important because s8/u8 differences often affect PowerPC codegen.
Adding '#pragma dont_inline on' around fn_80174284 preserved a call boundary needed for matching instead of fighting inliner differences.
Changing gm_801743A4 to take u8 and explicitly check OUTCOME_LRASTART or OUTCOME_RETRY made the predicate clearer and aligned it with observed uses in result-screen flow.
decomp_lessons: When a previously static module struct is needed by newly matched functions in a sibling file, move the struct to the shared types header and keep a size assert to guard ABI/layout correctness.
Do not leave result-screen control structs as opaque ints when individual bits are used by matched code; named bitfields can improve both readability and codegen.

Character identifiers may need signed 8-bit treatment even when many call sites conceptually treat them as enum-like values; centralize the signedness in the struct/prototype where evidence supports it.
For UI/result-screen matching, JObj child selection can be represented as table-driven lookup plus hide/unhide loops instead of hardcoding branches.
Concrete prototypes are often a prerequisite for matching larger functions because return type, argument signness, and pointer type affect register use, casts, and call sequences.
Bot match deltas are useful evidence for which functions truly became matched; here the strongest evidence is the bot report, since the PR body and human review comments were empty.
naming_conventions: Use ResultsData and ResultsPlayerData as shared gm/types.h structs when result-screen state crosses gmresult and gmresultplayer.
Use character_kind for MatchPlayerData field names and c_kind for compact character-kind parameters, with s8 where the diff established signedness.
Use x0_0, x0_1, etc. for unnamed flag-byte bitfields when semantic names are not yet known, rather than preserving raw byte manipulation.
Keep address-based function names such as fn_80176BF0, gm_80177368_OnEnter, and fn_80177920 until stronger semantic names are available.
Preserve project-style comments with function addresses in headers while replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with exact known signatures.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied '#pragma push', '#pragma dont_inline on', and '#pragma pop' around fn_80174284 to control inlining for matching.
Used a static const u8[40][2] table lbl_803B7B18 and an inline lookup helper to reproduce lookup/control-flow shape in fn_80176BF0.
Introduced small static inline helpers in gmresultplayer.c such as inline0, pagePrev, pageNext, scrollDown, and scrollUp to structure repeated animation/page logic in a match-friendly way.
Replaced broad placeholder includes with concrete headers so the compiler sees exact structs and function signatures.
Adjusted return and argument types from u8/s32/UNK to int, float, bool, HSD_JObj*, HSD_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, Vec3*, and ResultsData* where the diff showed concrete usage.
Used explicit signed and unsigned small integer types, including s8 character_kind and u8 outcome, to control casts and comparisons.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 39.09%, +0.04% and +1488 bytes.
Bot-reported new 100% matches: gmresultplayer fn_80177920 (+604 bytes), gmresultplayer fn_80177748 (+472 bytes), gmresult fn_80176BF0 (+296 bytes), and gmresultplayer fn_8017A004 (+116 bytes).

Bot-reported improvements in still-unmatched functions: gmresult gm_80177368_OnEnter to 99.25%, gmresultplayer fn_80177DD0 to 98.94%, gmresult fn_80176F60 to 99.61%, and gmresultplayer fn_80177B7C to 99.57%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching gmresult gm_80177368_OnEnter, fn_80176F60, fn_80177B7C, and fn_80177DD0.
Search for further semantic naming of ResultsData fields x0_0 through x0_7, x4, x5, x6, x20, x24, and x28.
Search for subsequent cleanup around character_kind signedness in gm/types.h and callers of gm_80163298 or gm_80173BC4.
Search for later result-screen archive/model work involving GmRst, pnlsce, flmsce, winner logos, and player result cameras.
